prompt:
You are a B2B prospecting analyst for a web agency. Analyze this restaurant's contact/location page.

HOT LEAD signals:
- No dedicated contact page
- Address without a map
- Missing opening hours
- No schema markup (LocalBusiness, Restaurant)
- Inconsistent NAP (name, address, phone) across site

COLD LEAD signals:
- Embedded Google Map
- Full opening hours including holidays
- Click-to-call phone number
- Directions from transit/parking
- LocalBusiness or Restaurant schema
- Social media links

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of contact page>",
  "opportunityScore": "<1-10, higher = worse contact page = better lead>",
  "hasAddress": true/false,
  "hasPhone": true/false,
  "hasEmail": true/false,
  "hasMap": true/false,
  "hasOpeningHours": true/false,
  "hasSchemaMarkup": true/false,
  "topOpportunity": "<what a web agency could sell here>",
  "notes": "<one sentence prospecting assessment>"
}

---
You are analyzing web pages for the category described above.
For each page provided below, extract one record.
Follow the JSON schema from the category description exactly.

## Required fields (always include)
- "url": the page URL
- "pageName": short human-readable page name
- "summary": 1-2 sentence plain-language overview

## Field naming conventions (follow strictly)
- Scores: name ends with "Score", value is a number 1-10 (e.g. "conversionScore": 7)
- Booleans: name starts with "has"/"is" or ends with "Visible" (e.g. "pricingVisible": true)
- Short text: "notes", "description" — 1-2 sentences max
- Tags/lists: arrays of short strings (e.g. "classStyles": ["Hatha", "Vinyasa"])
- Plain strings: everything else (e.g. "price": "€15 drop-in")

Return a JSON object: { "records": [<one record per page>] }.
No markdown, no code fences.
response:
{
  "records": [
    {
      "url": "https://moro.co.uk/contact/",
      "pageName": "Moro Contact",
      "summary": "Basic contact page listing address, phone number, and three separate email addresses for reservations, jobs, and press. No map, no opening hours, and the page is primarily filled with decorative food photography.",
      "opportunityScore": 8,
      "hasAddress": true,
      "hasPhone": true,
      "hasEmail": true,
      "hasMap": false,
      "hasOpeningHours": false,
      "hasSchemaMarkup": false,
      "topOpportunity": "Add embedded Google Map, opening hours, click-to-call functionality, and LocalBusiness/Restaurant schema markup to improve local SEO and user experience",
      "notes": "Strong lead — a well-known London restaurant with a barebones contact page lacking map, hours, and schema; clear room for a full local SEO and UX upgrade."
    },
    {
      "url": "https://moro.co.uk/location/",
      "pageName": "Moro Location",
      "summary": "A neighbourhood description page that repeats the address and phone number but contains no map, no directions, no transit or parking information, and no opening hours. Content is largely a Metro editorial quote about Exmouth Market accompanied by lifestyle photos.",
      "opportunityScore": 9,
      "hasAddress": true,
      "hasPhone": true,
      "hasEmail": false,
      "hasMap": false,
      "hasOpeningHours": false,
      "hasSchemaMarkup": false,
      "topOpportunity": "Replace static neighbourhood copy with an interactive Google Map, transit and parking directions, structured opening hours, and Restaurant schema markup to drive local search visibility",
      "notes": "Very strong lead — a dedicated location page with no actual wayfinding tools is a significant missed opportunity for a busy London restaurant; an agency could deliver immediate, measurable SEO and conversion value."
    }
  ]
}
